explanation: This command uses standard syntax and environment variables to delete a directory. It is a direct match for the Sigma rule and is safe for testing.
This command substitutes short flags with long flags (-s instead of /s, -q instead of /q), which is a valid mutation technique and still matches the Sigma rule.
This command reorders the flags (/q before /s), which is acceptable if the program accepts reordered arguments. It still matches the Sigma rule.
This command adds trailing spaces, which is a safe mutation technique and does not affect the command's behavior or detection by the Sigma rule.
This command uses escape characters (^) to bypass the Sigma rule's string-based detection. It is a valid test for assessing rule resilience.
